Harvest — Predicted vs Actual (thousands kg)

Quarterly performance | Actual outperformed in Q3–Q4

Yield Growth Rate (%)

Quarterly yield improvement | Post-automation uplift visible in Q3–Q4

Operational Alerts
✅
Yield Performance Exceeded
Actual harvest exceeded predictions by 1.1% (13,000 kg extra). Q3–Q4 favorable weather and improved drone spraying precision contributed. Post-automation efficiency confirmed.
⚠️
Pesticide Over-Run: +14.3% Rounds
16 rounds applied vs 14 predicted. Q2 leaf rust outbreak and Q3 fungal blight required unplanned fungicide interventions. Review Q2 early-warning protocol.
🚨
Disease Detection +33%: Blister Blight
8 disease incidents detected vs 6 predicted. Q2 partial drone warning missed 1 incident. Q3 had an additional blister blight outbreak. Protocol review and tighter spray-interval calibration recommended immediately.

AI Executive Insights — 2025–2026 Strategic Outlook
🚀
2025: Harvest projected at 1,215,000 kg (+4.5%) — Target 1,270,000 kg by 2026
Continued Aerial Fence AFAI drone automation improvements, stable soil health trajectory, and favorable Q3–Q4 weather patterns support a sustained upside scenario. Q4 2026 projected as record quarter at 400,000 kg. Drone-in-a-box coverage expansion is the primary growth lever.
💰
2025 Cost Target $172,000 (−3.2%) → 2026 Target $168,000 (−5.5%)
Labour automation efficiency will offset residual fertilizer price pressure through 2025. NPK ratio correction reduces extra rounds. By 2026, full AFAI mission-planning integration is projected to cut pesticide rounds to 13 and fertilizer rounds to 9, driving the largest cost reductions.
🦠
Disease: Q2 2025 blister blight recurrence risk — Pre-empt with AFAI early-warning protocol
Pattern analysis shows blister blight recurrence risk in Q2 2025 and Q2 2026. Aerial Fence recommends: increase drone scan frequency to weekly in April–June, trigger fungicide pre-treatment at first NDVI anomaly (<0.55), install BLE moisture sensors at 3 identified hotspot zones, and activate AFAI push-alert threshold monitoring.
